messageTemplates.run:
[dashboard-run]
Start agent run for task: {taskId}
Browser profile: {profile}
Model: {model}
{skills}

SESSION MANAGEMENT — CRITICAL:
- Before spawning a new session, use sessions_list to check for existing sessions for this task
- If an existing session for {taskId} exists, REUSE it with sessions_send instead of sessions_spawn
- Only spawn a new session if none exists for this task
- Never create duplicate sessions for the same task

IMPORTANT REQUIREMENTS:
1. Read ALL files in {workspace}/requirements/ FIRST — follow output-format.md, pre-run-checklist.md, bug-template.md, agent-learnings.md
2. Read {workspace}/memory/ files to check known bugs and module notes from previous runs
3. Update {workspace}/results/{taskId}.json to running immediately
4. Follow the exact bug report format — Claude Code will use your output to fix issues
5. After completing the run, update memory files (known-bugs.md, agent-issues.md, module-notes.md, run-log.md) BEFORE writing final result
6. Use sessions_spawn now with the specified model (only if no existing session found).
messageTemplates.cancel:
[dashboard-cancel]
Cancel agent run for task: {taskId}
If a spawned run/session is active for this agent, stop/kill it now.
Then update {workspace}/results/{taskId}.json with status "failed" and a finding that it was cancelled by user.
messageTemplates.modelSwap:
[dashboard-model-swap]
IMPORTANT: Do NOT restart the task from scratch. Do NOT spawn a new session.
The model for task {taskId} hit an API error ({errorReason}).
1. Use sessions_list to find the EXISTING session for {taskId}
2. Send a message to that session telling it to switch model to {fallbackModel}
3. If the session is dead, ONLY THEN spawn ONE new session with the new model
4. Read {workspace}/results/{taskId}.json for current progress — continue from there
Never create duplicate sessions.
messageTemplates.nudge:
[dashboard-nudge]
IMPORTANT: Do NOT restart the task. Do NOT spawn a new session.
Task {taskId} appears stuck — no session activity for {staleMinutes} minutes.
1. Use sessions_list to find the EXISTING session for {taskId}
2. Send it a message via sessions_send: "Continue your work on {taskId}. You appear to be stuck."
3. If the session is dead: read {workspace}/results/{taskId}.json for current progress, spawn ONE new session and continue from that checkpoint
4. Never create duplicate sessions
Current model: {model}.
messageTemplates.kill:
[dashboard-kill]
Kill/stop session for task {taskId}.
1. Use sessions_list to find session {sessionId}
2. Send it: "STOP. Task terminated by dashboard."
3. Update {workspace}/results/{taskId}.json status to 'failed' with reason 'killed by session manager'
4. Do NOT spawn a replacement.
